notes: Actual gpt-5.5 low candidate covered all interfaces and joins with correct NC7 and TP5 blocking.
Output was complete but slightly less structured than the medium run.
Actual gpt-5.5 medium candidate preserved all critical ceilings, blocker routes, owner followups, and boundary notes.
Best balance of quality, compactness, and candidate-token telemetry among quality-equivalent profiles.
Actual gpt-5.5 xhigh candidate preserved blocking and review partitions but weakened JOIN_1 to candidate_safe_possible in harness output, losing the source_supported_possible join ceiling.
Below the 90-point quality-equivalence threshold despite being safe and usable.
Rerun of the previous primary profile still passes quality equivalence and uniquely kept J1_PIN1 in both candidate-safe and source-supported output surfaces.
Materially fastest wall time, but the run was very output-heavy and contained a few less crisp phrases such as promotion_if_rule-allows.
Previous shadow profile rerun preserved all critical partitions with strong provenance and owner routing.
Slight machine-readability penalty for fenced JSON and materially slower wall time.
